← all tutorials

Session Guarantees (Bayou, 1994) — interactive 📖 SESSION_GUARANTEES.md 📄 session_guarantees.py

📖 Full guide  ·  📄 Ground-truth source — every panel below recomputes the same model in JS.
A session is one client's conversation with a replicated store. Eventual consistency only promises the replicas converge — nothing stops your own write from being invisible to your next read, or a value you watch from going backward. The four session guarantees (Terry et al. 1994) fix that with a little client-side bookkeeping. Toggle each one off and watch the violation appear; toggle it on and watch the fix (sticky route / block / buffer) kick in. The verifier is the byte-identical twin of session_guarantees.py, so the green check: OK below confirms a match.

① Client + 3 replicas — the session token flows with every op

What this shows: the client carries a session token — per-key last_write_version (RYW), highest_read_version (MR), write seq (MW), last_read_version (WFR). Click a card below to focus it here; the arrow goes red when the focused guarantee is OFF (violation) and green when ON (fix).
client (session) replica up-to-date replica lagging violating op guaranteed op

② The four guarantees — toggle each ON / OFF, watch violations come and go

each card recomputes its scenario live from the identical scripted inputs as session_guarantees.py Sections A–D
check: … JS verifier matches session_guarantees.py: RYW (need=1,have=0); MR (need=5,have=3); MW naive final=(1,1) seq [2,1]; WFR (X need=5,have=3)→Y=(10,7); all ON → 0 violations.
